title: English Cemetery, Surat
tagline: English Cemetery, Surat — heritage city of Gujarat

overview.short: British Cemetery of Surat also known as the English Cemetery, located at Katargam Darwaja in Surat, walking distance from the Dutch cemetery.
overview.description: British Cemetery of Surat also known as the English Cemetery, located at Katargam Darwaja in Surat, walking distance from the Dutch cemetery. The British Cemetery was established around 1651, and contains graves of former governors of Bombay Presidency and a number of officials of East India Company.

topPlaces.name: 1837 Surat fire
topPlaces.description: In April 1837, a fire broke out in the Indian city of Surat, then under British East India Company rule. It resulted in more than 500 deaths and the destruction of 9,737 houses in a 9+3⁄4-mile (16 km) radius.

topPlaces.name: Surat
topPlaces.description: Surat (Gujarati: [suɾət]) is a city in the western Indian state of Gujarat. The word Surat directly translates to face in Gujarati, Hindi and Urdu.

topPlaces.name: Expedition to Surat
topPlaces.description: The expedition to Surat was the failed establishment of a factory at Surat by the Swedish East India Company in April 1760. After being instigated by both British and Dutch authorities, the local Nawab sent Indian troops and occupied the fortress, holding Christopher Henric Braad and several of his companions captive for 20 days.

topPlaces.name: Surat Cantral GSRTC Bus Terminal
topPlaces.description: Surat Central GSRTC Bus Terminal or Surat Central Bus Station is the main bus terminal in Surat, India, for long-distance and inter-state buses operated by the Gujarat State Road Transport Corporation (GSRTC).

topPlaces.name: Saraswati Mandir
topPlaces.description: Saraswati Mandir, also known as Sarika Sadan or Narmad House, is a writer's house museum in Surat, Gujarat, India. It was built by Gujarati poet Narmad in 1866.

topPlaces.name: Katargam
topPlaces.description: Katargam is a suburb in Surat city and host of the world famous Surat diamond industry. Today Katargam is one of the best developed areas in Surat city and also boasts of diamond industries.

topPlaces.name: Morarji Desai Setu
topPlaces.description: Morarji Desai Setu is a weir-cum-causeway on the Tapi River in Surat, Gujarat, India. It connects Rander and Katargam neighbourhoods.

topPlaces.name: Gopi Talav
topPlaces.description: Gopi Talav or Gopi Lake is a lake in the Gopipura locality in the city of Surat in Gujarat state of India. It was built circa 1510 CE by Malik Gopi, who was an affluent merchant and governor of Surat during the Gujarat Sultanate.

items.note: In April 1837, a fire broke out in the Indian city of Surat, then under British East India Company rule. It resulted in more than 500 deaths and the destruction of 9,737 houses in a 9+3⁄4-mile (16 km) radius.
Surat (Gujarati: [suɾət]) is a city in the western Indian state of Gujarat. The word Surat directly translates to face in Gujarati, Hindi and Urdu.

The expedition to Surat was the failed establishment of a factory at Surat by the Swedish East India Company in April 1760. After being instigated by both British and Dutch authorities, the local Nawab sent Indian troops and occupied the fortress, holding Christopher Henric Braad and several of his companions captive for 20 days.

items.note: Surat Central GSRTC Bus Terminal or Surat Central Bus Station is the main bus terminal in Surat, India, for long-distance and inter-state buses operated by the Gujarat State Road Transport Corporation (GSRTC).
Saraswati Mandir, also known as Sarika Sadan or Narmad House, is a writer's house museum in Surat, Gujarat, India. It was built by Gujarati poet Narmad in 1866.
Katargam is a suburb in Surat city and host of the world famous Surat diamond industry. Today Katargam is one of the best developed areas in Surat city and also boasts of diamond industries.
Morarji Desai Setu is a weir-cum-causeway on the Tapi River in Surat, Gujarat, India. It connects Rander and Katargam neighbourhoods.

Gopi Talav or Gopi Lake is a lake in the Gopipura locality in the city of Surat in Gujarat state of India. It was built circa 1510 CE by Malik Gopi, who was an affluent merchant and governor of Surat during the Gujarat Sultanate.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit English Cemetery, Surat?
faq.a: The best time to visit English Cemetery, Surat is Oct – Mar. Summers are 28–44°C and winters are 8–25°C.

faq.q: How do I reach English Cemetery, Surat?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Surat Airport (14 km) and the nearest railway station is Surat Railway Station (2 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for English Cemetery, Surat?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of English Cemetery, Surat at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in English Cemetery, Surat?
faq.a: Stays in English Cemetery, Surat start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in English Cemetery, Surat?
faq.a: The top attractions are 1837 Surat fire, Surat, Expedition to Surat, Surat Cantral GSRTC Bus Terminal, Saraswati Mandir.
